Record Class Present<T>

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
io.github.igorgf.control.Present<T>
Type Parameters:
T - The contained value type.
Record Components:
value - The contained value. Never null.
All Implemented Interfaces:
Option<T>
record Present<T>(T value) extends Record implements Option<T>
The value presence representing implementation of Option.

Contains a NEVER null value.

See Also:
  • Option
  • Empty

  • Constructor Details

    • Present

      Present(T value)
      Creates an instance of a Present record class.
      Parameters:
      value - the value for the value record component

  • Method Details

    • map

      public <U, X extends Exception> Option<U> map(CheckedFunction<? super T, ? extends U, ? extends X> mapper) throws X
      The functor operation of Option. Transforms the contained value using the provided mapping function.

      When this is a Present<T> then the mapping is applied to the contained value, and contained within a new Present<U>, allowing for convenient chaining. Otherwise, this method returns a new Empty<U>.

      When the mapping has a chance of returning a null use Option.flatMap(CheckedFunction) instead, to return a Empty<U> for null result cases. If the mapping returns a null this method throws a NullPointerException.

      The mapper doesn't have to be concerned with handling null values as value() is never null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by mapper are propagated, see Option class documentation for details.

      Specified by:
      map in interface Option<T>
      Type Parameters:
      U - The type being contained after mapping.
      Parameters:
      mapper - The mapping function to apply to the contained value, if present.
      Returns:
      Present<U>
      Throws:
      X - If the mapper throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If mapper is null. Or if the mapping returns a null.
      See Also:
      • map(CheckedFunction)
      • Empty.map(CheckedFunction)

    • flatMap

      public <U, X extends Exception> Option<U> flatMap(CheckedFunction<? super T, ? extends Option<? extends U>, ? extends X> mapper) throws X
      The monad operation of Option. Transforms the contained value using the provided mapping function.

      This method is similar to Option.map(CheckedFunction), but the mapping function is one whose result is already an Option<? extends U>, and if invoked, flatMap does not wrap it within an additional Option.

      If the mapping returns a null then this method throws a NullPointerException. The mapper must always return a Option<? extends U>.

      The mapper doesn't have to be concerned with handling null values as value() is never null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by mapper are propagated, see Option class documentation for details.

      The unchecked cast from Option<? extends U> to Option<U> is provably safe because Option is covariant in its type parameter, it only produces values of U, never consumes them.

      Specified by:
      flatMap in interface Option<T>
      Type Parameters:
      U - The type being contained after mapping.
      Parameters:
      mapper - The mapping function to apply to the contained value, if present.
      Returns:
      Present<U>
      Throws:
      X - If the mapper throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If mapper is null. Or if the mapping returns a null.
      See Also:
      • Option.map(CheckedFunction)
      • flatMap(CheckedFunction)
      • Empty.flatMap(CheckedFunction)

    • fold

      public <U, X1 extends Exception, X2 extends Exception> U fold(CheckedFunction<? super T, ? extends U, ? extends X1> presentMapper, CheckedSupplier<? extends U, ? extends X2> emptySupplier) throws X1
      The catamorphism operation of Option. Transforms and extracts the contained value using the provided mapping function when this is a Present. Supplies a value when this is a Empty.

      If the mapper or the supplier return a null then this method throws a NullPointerException. The mapper and supplier must always return a U. When the mapping has a chance of returning a null use Option.flatMap(CheckedFunction) instead, to return a Option<U>, to then handle the result using Option.orElseGet(CheckedSupplier).

      The presentMapper doesn't have to be concerned with handling null values as value() is never null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by presentMapper or emptySupplier are propagated, see Option class documentation for details.

      Specified by:
      fold in interface Option<T>
      Type Parameters:
      U - The type being contained after mapping.
      Parameters:
      presentMapper - The mapping function to apply to the contained value, if this is a Present.
      emptySupplier - The supplying function to provide a replacement value, if this is a Empty.
      Returns:
      U from the presentMapper.
      Throws:
      X1 - If the presentMapper throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If presentMapper is null or it returns a null.
      See Also:
      • fold(CheckedFunction, CheckedSupplier)
      • Empty.fold(CheckedFunction, CheckedSupplier)

    • isPresent

      public boolean isPresent()
      Is this a Present.
      Specified by:
      isPresent in interface Option<T>
      Returns:
      true
      See Also:
      • Option.isEmpty()

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Is this a Empty.
      Specified by:
      isEmpty in interface Option<T>
      Returns:
      false
      See Also:
      • Option.isPresent()

    • ifPresent

      public <X extends Exception> void ifPresent(CheckedConsumer<? super T, ? extends X> action) throws X
      If this is a Present, performs the given action with the value, otherwise does nothing.

      The action doesn't have to be concerned with handling null values as value() is never null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by action are propagated, see Option class documentation for details.

      Specified by:
      ifPresent in interface Option<T>
      Parameters:
      action - the action to be performed, if this is a Present
      Throws:
      X - If the action throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If action is null.

    • ifEmpty

      public <X extends Exception> void ifEmpty(CheckedRunnable<? extends X> emptyAction)
      Do nothing because this is a Present.
      Specified by:
      ifEmpty in interface Option<T>
      Parameters:
      emptyAction - the action to be performed, if this is a Empty

    • ifPresentOrElse

      public <X extends Exception> void ifPresentOrElse(CheckedConsumer<? super T, ? extends X> action, CheckedRunnable<? extends X> emptyAction) throws X
      If this is a Present, performs the given action with value, otherwise performs the empty case action.

      The action doesn't have to be concerned with handling null values as value() is never null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by action or emptyAction are propagated, see Option class documentation for details.

      Specified by:
      ifPresentOrElse in interface Option<T>
      Parameters:
      action - the action to be performed, if this is a Present
      emptyAction - the action to be performed, if this is a Empty
      Throws:
      X - If the action throws a checked exception, it is propagated to the caller.
      NullPointerException - If action is null.

    • filter

      public Option<T> filter(Predicate<? super T> predicate)
      Facilitates conditional short-circuiting. If this is a Present, and the value matches the given predicate, returns a this, otherwise returns a new Empty<T>.

      The predicate doesn't have to be concerned with handling null values as value() is never null.

      Specified by:
      filter in interface Option<T>
      Parameters:
      predicate - The predicate to apply to a value, if this is a Present
      Returns:
      Present<T> if this is a Present, and the value matches the given predicate. Otherwise Empty<T>.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - If the predicate is null.

    • orElse

      public T orElse(T other)
      Description copied from interface: Option
      If this is a Present returns contained value. Otherwise, returns other.

      Never returns null, as value() and other are never null. For null result support use Option.orElseNullable(Object).

      Specified by:
      orElse in interface Option<T>
      Parameters:
      other - A non-null T to be returned if this is a Empty.
      Returns:
      Contained value.
      See Also:
      • Option.orElseNullable(Object)

    • orElseNullable

      public T orElseNullable(T other)
      Description copied from interface: Option
      If this is a Present returns contained value. Otherwise, returns other, which may be null.

      This is the sole nullable exit point in the Option API. The entire API otherwise rejects null at every boundary: constructors, mapping functions, and suppliers all throw NullPointerException on null. This method, alongside Option.ofNullable(Object) as its nullable entry counterpart, forms the deliberate and exclusive pair of escape hatches for interoperation with null-returning Java APIs. Prefer Option.orElse(Object) in all other cases.

      Specified by:
      orElseNullable in interface Option<T>
      Parameters:
      other - T to be returned if this is a Empty, may be null.
      Returns:
      Contained value.
      See Also:
      • Option.ofNullable(Object)
      • Option.orElse(Object)

    • orElseGet

      public <X extends Exception> T orElseGet(CheckedSupplier<? extends T, ? extends X> supplier)
      Description copied from interface: Option
      If this is a Present, returns contained value. Otherwise, uses the supplier to return an instance of T.

      Never returns null, as value() and supplier return values are never null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by supplier are propagated, see Option class documentation for details.

      Specified by:
      orElseGet in interface Option<T>
      Parameters:
      supplier - Used to provide the replacement T when this is a Empty.
      Returns:
      Contained value.

    • orElseThrow

      public <X extends Exception> T orElseThrow(Supplier<? extends X> exceptionSupplier)
      Description copied from interface: Option
      If this is a Present, returns contained value. Otherwise, uses the supplier to get an exception, then throws it.

      Never returns null, as value() is never null.

      Checked exceptions thrown by exceptionSupplier are propagated, see Option class documentation for details.

      Specified by:
      orElseThrow in interface Option<T>
      Parameters:
      exceptionSupplier - Used to provide the exception to throw when this is a Empty.
      Returns:
      Contained value.

    • orThrow

      public T orThrow()
      Description copied from interface: Option
      If this is a Present, returns contained value. Otherwise, throws a checked EmptyValueException.

      Never returns null, as value() and other are never null.

      Specified by:
      orThrow in interface Option<T>
      Returns:
      Contained value.

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. All components in this record class are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object).
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.

    • value

      public T value()
      Returns the value of the value record component.
      Returns:
      the value of the value record component
